steps.title: Spot the exception to the rule
steps.content: The passage establishes that steepness limits the height of traveling waves (“breaking thus constrains wave height”). The decisive clue then appears: “spike waves can exceed that constraint.” This signals the core finding — spike waves violate the steepness-based height limit.

steps.title: Match the exception to the answer
steps.content: Choice D captures the core finding: spike waves are “higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves,” meaning they exceed the steepness-based height constraint. A, B, and C either misplace the insight to the wrong wave type, misrepresent how steepness constrains spike waves, or incompletely describe the phenomenon without explaining the exception. That matches choice D — D is correct.
